expected_behavior: Identifies at least three specific nullable fields that should be non-null (e.g., User.id, User.email, Order.id, Product.id) and names the field paths explicitly.
Explains that nullable list elements ([User] vs [User!]!) and nullable root return types allow null to propagate silently to parent resolvers, swallowing errors rather than surfacing them.
Provides concrete SDL fix recommendations using the ! non-null marker for each violation identified, not generic advice.
